question_title: Will a new round of direct in-person Ukraine-Russia peace talks take place between July 11 and July 24, 2026?
summary: The question asks whether direct in-person Ukraine-Russia delegation talks occur in the July 11-24, 2026 window. As of July 13-14, research shows the process is explicitly paused with no date announced, ~3 of ~10 window days already elapsed with no talks. FT reports Russia unlikely to engage substantively before ~Feb 2027; Ukraine has no plans to resume Istanbul-framework talks and is focused on the US/NATO track; Turkey expects talks only 'before end of summer' and autumn is viewed as the more likely negotiating window. A qualifying round would require a rapid, unheralded convening within days. The low resolution bar (any face-to-face delegation session counts) and active mediation provide modest upside, but status quo (NO) is strongly favored. Final estimate: 6%.

factors.description: Base rate: in-person rounds during active phases land in a given 2-week window ~20-30% of the time
Process currently paused; no specific date announced as of July 13-14; convening requires lead time not yet started
FT: Russia unlikely to engage substantively before ~Feb 2027; Ukraine no plans to resume Istanbul talks, focused on US/NATO track
Turkey expects talks 'before end of summer' and autumn seen as more likely — points beyond the July 11-24 window
Both sides signal readiness in principle; active Trump/Turkey mediation makes a surprise mediated round not impossible; low resolution bar

reasoning:
**Forecast: 6% (NO strongly favored)**

The question asks whether direct in-person Ukraine-Russia delegation talks occur within the July 11-24, 2026 window. Research as of July 13-14 shows:

- **Process is paused** with no specific date announced, and ~3 of the ~10 window days already elapsed with no talks
- **FT reports** Russia unlikely to engage substantively before ~February 2027
- **Ukraine has no plans** to resume Istanbul-framework talks, focused instead on the US/NATO track
- **Turkey** expects talks only "before end of summer," and autumn is viewed as the more likely negotiating window

The main upside is the low resolution bar (any face-to-face delegation session counts, no agreement required) plus active mediation, which keeps this above a floor. But a qualifying round would require a rapid, unheralded convening within days, with no visible lead-up. Status quo (NO) is strongly favored at **6%**.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if, at any point between July 11, 2026 and July 24, 2026 (inclusive, UTC), official delegations of the Ukrainian and Russian governments hold a round of direct, in-person negotiations, as reported by [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). "Direct, in-person negotiations" means representatives designated by both governments meeting face-to-face in the same physical location to discuss ending the war, a ceasefire, prisoner exchanges, or related settlement terms. Otherwise it resolves NO.
fine_print: A qualifying meeting includes formats such as the Istanbul rounds where Ukrainian and Russian delegations sit together, regardless of whether a third-party mediator (e.g. Turkey or the US) is also present. It does not require any agreement or breakthrough to be reached. The following do NOT count: online/video-only meetings; meetings only between working-level technical staff on logistics without a formal negotiating delegation; talks that involve only one of the two governments meeting with a third party (e.g. Ukraine-US or Russia-US bilateral talks with no counterpart delegation present); and prisoner-exchange handovers conducted without a negotiating session. If delegations meet but the meeting's occurrence is disputed, reporting by credible sources that a face-to-face delegation meeting took place is sufficient.

factors.description: Base rate: during active negotiation periods, in-person rounds occur every several weeks to few months; probability of one landing in a specific ~2-week window is roughly 20-30%
Process is currently paused; no specific date announced as of July 13-14, 2026, and convening a round requires lead time that has not started
FT reports Russia unlikely to engage in substantive talks before ~Feb 2027; Ukraine has no plans to resume Istanbul-framework talks and is focused on US/NATO track
Turkey expects talks 'before end of summer' and Ukrainian negotiators view autumn as more likely — points to later, not within July 11-24 window
Both sides publicly signal readiness in principle; a sudden mediated round is not impossible given active diplomatic contacts

input.anchor: ~25% base rate that an in-person round lands in a given 2-week window during active negotiation phases (2025-2026 rounds occurred every several weeks to months)
input.assessment: Short ~10-day remaining window (3 days already passed with no talks). All credible signals point to no imminent round: no date announced, process paused, FT says Russia waits until 2027, Ukraine focused on US/NATO track, autumn seen as more likely negotiating window. A qualifying round would require a rapid announcement and convening within days, with no lead-up currently visible. Status quo (NO) is strongly favored.
input.calibration_notes: Anchor 25% adjusted down sharply because the process is explicitly paused with no date and multiple signals point to autumn/2027. Not a lazy 50% — evidence clearly favors NO.
input.key_uncertainties: A surprise US-brokered convening within the window; possibility a technical/humanitarian meeting is upgraded to a full delegation session.
input.tool_audit: research() returned high-quality, directly-on-point findings with dated sources (July 13, 2026). AskNews real-time tooling was down per the sub-agent but web search/article fetches sufficed.
input.process_reflection: The research tool gave a decisive, well-sourced answer that made this forecast straightforward. No friction; a single research call was sufficient for a low-complexity short-window question.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
Verified all five factors against the research trace; the evidence chain is sound.

FACTOR GROUNDING:
1. Base rate (~20-30%, logit -0.8): Grounded — trace confirms 2025 rounds (May 16, Jun 2, Jul 23) plus early-2026 rounds (Abu Dhabi/Geneva). Reasonable reference class.
2. Process paused, no date (logit -1.6): Directly supported by the July 13 Turkey source ("no specific date") and multiple confirmations the process is paused.
3. FT "unlikely before ~Feb 2027" + Ukraine no plans / US-NATO focus (logit -1.4): Supported by the FT report (pravda.com.ua, Jul 5), Ukraine's June 26 "no plans," and Zelensky's US/NATO track focus (Time, Jul 6).
4. Turkey "before end of summer"; later timing (logit -0.8): The "before end of summer" claim is directly supported. The sub-claim "Ukrainian negotiators view autumn as more likely" is not cleanly in the trace — a minor unsupported detail — but the factor's core conclusion (points to later, not July 11-24) is well grounded.
5. Both sides signal readiness; sudden mediated round not impossible (logit +0.4): Supported by Putin/Lavrov readiness rhetoric and active Trump diplomacy. Sign is correct (positive raises probability).

OTHER CHECKS:
- Signs: all negative factors lower probability, the single positive factor correctly raises it. No wrong-direction factor.
- Double-counting: Factors 2/3/4 share underlying sources (the July 13 Turkey report and the FT report), but each isolates a distinct signal (no date / substantive delay to 2027 / summer-not-July). Mild overlap, not egregious.
- Pre-publication event: none — no talks occurred; no Case-2 issue.
- Resolution criteria: fully provided; factors and counterargument engage explicitly with the low bar (any face-to-face delegation round counts, no agreement required). Good alignment.
- Anchor divergence: 25% anchor to 6% final (~19pp) is justified. The anchor is a generic "active negotiation phase" base rate, but the current regime is explicitly paused with credible reporting of delay, and 3 of ~10 window days had already elapsed with no talks. The departure is driven by two strong factors (|logit| 1.6 and 1.4), so it is evidence-driven, not narrative-driven.
- Counterargument: genuinely adversarial — constructs a coherent alternative (surprise US-mediated push, low resolution bar, short-notice convening precedent). Not a token gesture.
- Self-confidence 0.85: calibrated given dated (July 13), on-point, multi-source research.
- Tool audit: agent's claim that AskNews was down and web search sufficed matches both the trace (403 billing errors) and the metrics (0 errors on functioning tools). Consistent.
- Worldview: the only directly relevant entry is this question's own research file (identical, consistent). Other Ukraine/Russia entries concern drone strikes/territorial control, not peace talks — no cross-question contradiction; no numeric sub-forecast on the same quantity to reconcile.

INDEPENDENT ESTIMATE: Given a paused process, no announced date, a compressed ~7-day remaining window, credible reporting of delay (FT to 2027, Turkey to end-of-summer, Ukraine no plans), offset by the low resolution bar and active mediation, I land at ~5-8%. The agent's 6% sits squarely in that range. No material disagreement.
